verdict: INCONCLUSIVE_DATA_PENDING — no outcome variable loaded; missing: ['oecd_lfs:lfpr_25_54', 'ilo:emp_15_64']
falsification_rule_text: SUPPORTED if β1 (transfers without incentives) is negative and significant at p<0.10 while β2 (transfers with incentives) is insignificant or less negative. PARTIAL if both are negative but |β1| > |β2|. REFUTED if β2 is negative and significantly larger than β1 (work incentives backfire) or if both are positive. INFORMATIVE: the male prime-age subsample should show the same pattern as the full sample; if only females drive the result, the mechanism is family-structure rather than work-incentive design.

variables_missing.source: constructed: social transfers excluding EITC/NIT equivalents / GDP, interacted with benefit-replacement-rate proxy

variables_missing.source: constructed: EITC/NIT-equivalent spending + ALMP spending / GDP
